title: Lap 3 — Dynatrace in Production
imagine: You are now the go-to Dynatrace expert on your team. Time to showcase you can investigate a problem in Dynatrace.
yourGoal: Complete 6 questions covering Problem analysis. These are the skills that separate Dynatrace power users from everyone else.
tools: Dynatrace Perform · Dynatrace Intelligence · Workflows · Dashboards
note: These questions simulate an on-call incident investigation using a real Dynatrace Problem card. You are expected to quickly assess blast radius, trust Davis AI’s entity-level root cause, understand why baselines triggered an anomaly, and use Explain Problem to align investigation and communication. The goal is speed with confidence.

story.introduction: You have crawled, you have walked — now you run. This scenario covers the advanced capabilities that make Dynatrace a platform, not just a monitoring tool.
story.context: From DQL-powered log queries to automated remediation workflows, this is where Dynatrace transforms from a tool you use into a platform your organisation depends on.

questions.title: Problem impact summary
questions.content: From the Dynatrace problem card, which combination correctly summarises the observed impact during the problem?

options.text: Approximately 1.6K users, 1.59K sessions, and 3 events
options.explanation: Correct. The problem card shows ~1.6K impacted users, ~1.59K sessions, and 3 correlated events.

options.text: Approximately 2.59K users, 1.6K sessions, and 1 event
options.explanation: Users and sessions are reversed, and the event count is incorrect.

options.text: 50 users, 123 sessions, and 103 events
options.explanation: These values correspond to baseline and metric values, not impact statistics.

options.text: Impact details cannot be determined from the problem card
options.explanation: The impact section of the problem card explicitly shows users, sessions, and event count.

questions.explanation: The impact tiles on the problem card provide a quick summary of blast radius before deeper analysis.
hints: Look at the impact tiles at the top of the problem card for the details.

questions.title: Root cause identification
questions.content: In the same problem, what application was identified by Davis AI as the root cause of the problem?

options.text: The 'Application low traffic' event
options.explanation: This is an informational event, not an entity root cause.

options.text: The 'Request error rate increase' anomaly
options.explanation: This describes the symptom, not the underlying root cause entity.

options.text: The web application DXP AAMI PERF2
options.explanation: Correct. The problem card explicitly labels DXP AAMI PERF2 as the root cause entity.

options.text: A backend service dependency
options.explanation: No service was shown as the root cause for this problem.

questions.explanation: Dynatrace always assigns root cause to an entity (application, service, host), not just an event or metric.
hints: Focus on which entity is explicitly labelled as the root causem, it would be default appear on the right side of the problem.

questions.title: Baseline-driven anomaly detection
questions.content: Why was the 'Request error rate increase' problem opened?

options.text: The error rate crossed a manually configured static threshold
options.explanation: This problem was detected using automatic baselining, not static thresholds.

options.text: The current error rate value of 123 exceeded the auto-baseline of 103.18
options.explanation: Correct. Dynatrace detected an anomaly because the observed value was above the learned baseline.

options.text: The request volume dropped below 50 requests per minute
options.explanation: This explains the low traffic event, not the error rate increase.

options.text: A deployment event automatically opened the problem
options.explanation: Deployments alone do not open problems unless they cause observable impact.

questions.explanation: Dynatrace compares live values against dynamic baselines learned from historical behaviour to detect anomalies.

questions.title: Using Explain Problem
questions.content: How is the 'Explain problem' feature most useful during investigation?

options.text: It replaces the need to look at metrics, logs, or traces
options.explanation: Explain Problem complements investigation but does not replace detailed analysis.

options.text: It provides an AI-generated summary explaining what happened, why it happened, and what was impacted
options.explanation: Correct. Explain Problem summarises the situation and guides users toward the most relevant evidence.

options.text: It automatically remediates the issue
options.explanation: Remediation requires Workflows or manual action, not Explain Problem.

options.text: It only displays raw event logs
options.explanation: Explain Problem focuses on understanding, not raw data display.

questions.explanation: Explain Problem helps teams align quickly by providing a clear narrative of cause, impact, and evidence — especially useful during handovers or incident reviews.
hints: Think about how you would quickly brief another engineer on what happened without walking them through every chart.

questions.title: Identifying the root cause service for an infrastructure problem.
questions.content: Open the above problem card titled 'Unexpected high load'. From this problem, which entity has been identified by Davis AI as the root cause?

options.text: oradb-app-ccm-sys-3 because it shows resource contention
options.explanation: This service is impacted, but it is not marked as the root cause.

options.text: ccbatch because a deployment changed
options.explanation: The process change is informational and not marked as the root cause.

options.text: CC Guidewire Batch Message Dispatcher due to unexpected high service load
options.explanation: Correct. This service is explicitly labelled as the root cause with the reason 'Unexpected high service load'.

options.text: The infrastructure host associated with the batch jobs
options.explanation: Infrastructure is impacted, but the problem root cause is identified at the service level.

questions.explanation: Davis AI identifies CC Guidewire Batch Message Dispatcher as the root cause service, allowing you to focus investigation where the abnormal load originated.
hints: Look for the entity that has a red 'Root cause' label, not just impacted components.

learningObjectives: Use DQL to query and analyse logs at scale
Understand why Davis Security Score differs from CVSS and how to use it for prioritisation
Calculate and interpret SLO error budgets
Understand how Workflows enable automated remediation
Distinguish between Dynatrace Problems and Events
Configure log attributes for efficient filtering
